item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image used here was created by author spikebrennan and as such is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it a dead-horse beating to want Doctor King to fill it with bottles of apple seeds?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good idea, interesting logs (especially the daevite bit), but the description is a bit lacking.
Do the bottles empty themselves or do you have to empty all of them before running a new test? Can several subjects relive a memory after one subject touches the glass? Do gloves prevent you from reliving a memory? Do you have to work in gloves for safety? What happens if you touch the glass and touch it again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Subject's last chronological recollection was of a "Daeva attack", after which subject could not recall any subsequent events.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is it tagged as "daevite"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "The Marcinkiewicz-Zygmund integral and its relation to Burkill’s SCP-integral" is an article published in the Moscow University Mathematics Bulletin. Just so you know…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I rather enjoyed the article myself so +1
However, I was not aware that this was in any way connected to SCP-914 until I read the discussion for this. The execution was pretty alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is now a regular, mainlist SCP article.

And the logs are explicitly opened up as collaborations.

item.title: Re: Issue w.r.t. Links
item.content:encoded: Are you sure? I've checked the mainlist and the entry for 2914 is valid and it does lead to the contest hub.

item.title: Re: Issue w.r.t. Links
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2914 does not, currently, exist. The 2914 we are commenting on is a reimagining of SCP-914 (which I did not perceive from the article itself either). I'm personally of the idea it COULD become a mainlist item. I like the concept a lot, but feel the connection to the source is tenuous at best.

Still +1.

item.title: Issue w.r.t. Links
item.content:encoded: I wish to highlight the following issue: When I clicked the entry for SCP-2914 from the Series III mainlist, it redirects me directly to the Reimagining Contest hub. Shouldn't the correct link be the SCP-2914 article itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same concerns than Decibelles and TwistedGears. However, I do like the writing at points, and do not mind the imagery. Otherwise, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since one's time in the womb is not generally something one remembers, I can't help but second guess the amniotic fluid bit. Curious…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this, and the first thought I had was, 'meh'. It seems like an good concept, and I could see the connection between this and 914, with refining items; however, this doesn't seem to refine a memory, just materialize it. Maybe have the memory of the individuals altered or improved in some way. Just a suggestion.

The flow seemed to change throughout the article. It was serious in some places, but got a bit too lighthearted in others. I would prefer to see a more established and consistent flow.

I couldn't see any grammar/spelling errors in my first read-through, good job on that.

No vote for now, but I would love to see what it could become.

Side Note: The tabs didn't do much for me, but that's just a personal preference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rodents have a knack for getting into places where you don't want them. They have rodent problems on aircraft carriers, and those are mostly made of metal, have water tight doors between most rooms, and rarely enter port.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually really dig this; its a creative effect with some nice deep testing logs. Despite the protests of the jaded long-timers, personally I think we don't see enough of this sort of SCP: concepts that stand on their own without the need for a lot of story to prop them up, and the foundation actually taking the time to try and figure some of these out rather than ignoring them and hoping they go away.

Not sure what the lolFoundation/incompetence thing is that people are referring to, and its possible that its been changed by the time I read this. If its the last one in the reconstruction logs, though, I'd hardly call the escape of a clearly highly intelligent D-class with at least partial access to the memories of an upper-clearance researcher incompetence. There have been successful escapes from maximum security prisons with less than that to work with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That seems like a really trivial gripe to me, but to each his own I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The rat droppings in his lunch seems unnecessary. They're just thrown in for squick, and go against the idea of Foundation sites being secure and professional environments. Letting small animals anywhere in an anomoly storage area is just asking for trouble.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It could stand as an scp but not entirely sure it works as a reimagining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "tale" tag is added because for technical reasons, it's the only way to make it show up on the contest entry page. It should be removed when the contest ends.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much what a lot of people are saying. Good SCP, but I didn't see any connection before the comments and think it's probably tenuous at best. +1 as an SCP, but not as a contest entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see the connection to 914, at all, but this is a decent skip.
You get a neutral vote.

Also, This is the first time I see "RECORD REMOVED". Is this the new [DATA EXPUNGED]?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, I'm just struggling to make the connections to 914 when I think they should be more intrinsic.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not liking this as much as I didn't realize this was a reimagining contest entry and was getting the vibes of an anomalous item. Not really warranting an upvote, but I liked the concept of re/deconstructing memories so a neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This needs to be expanded before I can vote in its favor. You have a few interesting things here, but not enough to really make me dig it. What I really want to see is this becoming a semi-open test log like 914 itself is, because that would be pretty cool.

No vote, may upvote once this has some more meat on its bones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't get my upvote until the third log. That's a really fascinating way to go about testing an object. Though, like Mann's 076 thing, I have to question what the connection is here, and whether I even understand what this contest is supposed to be about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough, still a -1. Like I said though, I do really like this idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The conceit is that whereas 914 on its "rough" or "coarse" settings can be used to physically de-construct a tangible object (and also works in reverse after a fashion), 2914 can be used to physically de-construct a human memory (and also works in reverse after a fashion).

When I discussed this with others, I got reactions ranging from "it's too much of a re-hash of 914" to "it's nothing like 914" so I figure that the truth is arguably somewhere in between those extremes. Your mileage may vary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the concept here, and would like to see it refined as a mainlist entry, however I am downvoting because I feel that the concept should have been expanded on and explored more, both in and out of the test logs. I also have a hard time seeing this as a reimagining of 914, the only similarities that I can see are "makes stuff" and "has extended test logs."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whether this is a standalone article or a contest entry, I can't support it.

It's not interesting. My first time reading through it, I couldn't even remember much about it, and there's nothing to keep it interesting throughout. It ends in a pretty frustrating way, with expungements that don't add, but detract from the article and I feel like whatever kind of narrative you were attempting to build (which I didn't see much of) failed. True, SCP-914 doesn't have much of a story either, but it's interesting and even outside of the test logs it's memorable.

Speaking about this as a contest entry: until I read the comments and saw what you said on IRC, I wasn't even aware this was a reimagination of SCP-914. The connection is tenuous, at best, and completely missable at worst. Even moreso, each tab has to stand on its own in order to hold its own as an entry. If one tab doesn't hold up on its own much, it's expected that the other two should. That being said, nothing except the first tab holds up much on its own. This isn't breaking rules as far as contest entries go, I'm sure, but I feel like it goes against the spirit of reimagining an skip in the first place and a quick way to fulfill requirements while not expanding much. You could have taken something uninteresting to make interesting and give it a story, but just fill it with test logs and make it boring as a result.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As an scp I do like it. By the sound of it it takes all points and makes a best fit line for what should happen.

➕ 1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeahhhhh, no, not buying this. As an SCP or as a contest entry.

As an SCP, it's little more than a memory sharing thing ending in Foundation incompetence and what is basically an expunge. Not a fan.

As a contest entry, I can't say this even qualifies. Chopping up the SCP into three tabs by just separating test logs in incredibly lazy and, so far as I can tell, completely ignores the reason for that limitation in the first place. So now each tab doesn't stand on its own, which they should.

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who's my Reimagining Contest entry? Who's a re-imagination of SCP-914? You are! Yes you are! That's a good boy!

Results here: W00T LAST PLACE!
